differentials.name: thoracic outlet syndrome (due to elevation of floor of scalene triangle with decrease of costoclavicular space):
differentials.raw: thoracic outlet syndrome (due to elevation of floor of scalene triangle with decrease of costoclavicular space):

differentials.name: Forked / bifid rib (0.6%) = duplication of anterior portion
differentials.raw: Forked / bifid rib (0.6%) = duplication of anterior portion

context: Prevalence:  1.4% M< F ◊  10-20% of symptomatic patients have a responsible cervical rib ◊  5-10% of complete cervical ribs cause symptoms May be associated with:   Klippel-Feil anomaly √  uni- / bilateral √  may fuse with first ribs anteriorly √  adjacent transverse process angulated inferiorly Cx:   aneurysmal dilatation of subclavian a. DDx:   elongated transverse process of 7th cervical vertebra; hypoplastic 1st thoracic rib Location:   4th rib (most often) May be associated with:   Gorlin basal cell nevus syndrome ◊  A single bifid rib is most commonly a normal incidental finding!
